item.title: Baby sloth reunites with his mother.
item.dc:creator: VioletBunnyDraws

item.title: One upside to having ADHD is that you gain a new hobby/obsession every week! Here are some of mine:
item.description:
Post apocalyptic cosplay will always have a dirty place in my heart
I love to draw and I was blown away when an Imgurian recently reached out to buy a tattoo commission from me! My art is going to be on someone's body forever!
My last secret Santa where I made a viking style set of coasters and drinking horn holster. Leather work is probably my most rewarding passion.
I try my best at baking but it is definitely a science. Here's a Portal cake replica I made for a birthday party.
Probably my SO's favorite of my hobbies. Understandable since they get to eat the rejects AND the winners.
Bartender by trade. Edible art is great when you can see the transition of happiness in people when they get to taste your concoctions.
I love to grow plants and mushrooms alike! I had to fill my home with greenery since I live in the desert.
Needle felting for when I feel like making cute tiny things. I made a replica of my axolotl
Camping is an essential break from the dread I start to feel when work and too many horrible news stories start to pile up. 5/7 would recommend.
This is Valley Of Fire State Park, NV. One of my top 3 favorite places to go.
Hammy the axolotl tax!
item.dc:creator: LANAAAAAAAAAA

item.title: Doctor Creates Pakistan’s First Insulin Patch System For Diabetic Patients
item.description: Led by Dr. Talha Durrani from Peshawar’s Khyber Teaching Hospital (KTH), a team of doctors, engineers, and data scientists, has indigenously developed a painless insulin delivery device using transdermal patches. If produced on a large scale, the device has the potential to replace the traditional injections/pens used to inject insulin into the body. Sauce: https://propakistani.pk/2022/02/14/doctor-creates-pakistans-first-insulin-patches-for-diabetic-patients/
item.dc:creator: PoorSucker

item.title: Did this. Worked with Comcast. Gave me two years worth of credit.
item.description: I called Comcast and told them I wanted Fox removed. They said it was a core channel and couldn’t be removed so I demanded to not pay for it. I was given a 2 year credit for the cost of the single channel. Only about $20 per year but it’s not about the money, it’s about sending a message. Front page edit: YAY, front page. The main question is who still has cable: and I still have cable so I can record sports, watch on my schedule and watch without commercials. Nothing like watching an NFL game in 47 min. If someone has a way I can watch sports not live on a streaming service or internet I’m all ears.
item.dc:creator: SchenectadySlumlord

item.title: Day 355: of cheeseburgers I want inside of me
item.description: Braised Short Rib Beef Burger Canadian 4oz Beef Patty, Slow-braised Boneless Canadian Beef Short Rib, Crispy Fried Onions, Arugula, Tomato, Mayo & Caramelized Onion Bacon Jam From White Spot In Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada Thank You @RobDangle For This Burger Submission! 13/10 Previous Day 354: https://imgur.com/gallery/yuUkUdR MESSAGE ME BURGER PICS OR YOUR FAVORITE BURGER SPOTS! Map of all burger spots that are available on yelp! Big thanks to @OhBoyDoIHaveNews4U for this. Link to his post and map https://imgur.com/gallery/vcObDuD @CrisprCAS Daily Tag 10 Days Remain

item.title: Brothers in Arms - note the matching cover pages as well as shirts & ties
item.description:
This was well choreographed delivery of Finland's and Sweden's NATO applications.
Finally! To my Swedish friends, as a Finn: Good luck with the ice hockey game later today. To older NATO members, I hope we'll be brothers soon enough. Unity is a good thing in fast changing world.
item.dc:creator: tennessine
